subject: Computer Networking
title: Zero-Day Vulnerabilities
description: A quick, practical guide to Zero-Day Vulnerabilities.

hook.text: A flaw exists in software that nobody knows about — except the attacker. That's a zero-day.

buildup.text: Zero-day means zero days of warning. There's no patch available because the vendor doesn't know yet.

discovery.text: Governments and criminal groups stockpile zero-days. Some sell for millions on dark markets.

twist.text: Stuxnet used four zero-days at once to sabotage Iranian nuclear centrifuges. The most sophisticated cyberweapon ever.

climax.text: Bug bounty programs pay researchers to find and report vulnerabilities before attackers do. Prevention beats cure.

punchline.text: Zero-days are the unknown unknowns. You can't patch what you can't see.

quiz.question: What makes a zero-day vulnerability dangerous?
options: No patch exists because the vendor is unaware of it
It only affects devices manufactured on a specific day
It requires physical access to exploit
